news.title: World Cup 2026: What is the perfect age to win the tournament?
news.lede: Of the 22 teams to have won the World Cup, 19 of them had an average age between 26 and 28 when doing so. That will be welcome news to England, France and Spain, whose average ages of their starting 11s at this World Cup all fit that age bracket. If you listed the average starting ages of the 22 World Cup-winning teams of the past, the median average is 27 years and 57 days old. Should Argentina go on to win the tournament, they would do so as one of the oldest sides to become World Cup champions.

news.title: 6 million sign petition to kick Argentina out of World Cup, citing favouritism
news.lede: More than six million people have signed an online petition demanding Argentina’s expulsion from the World Cup, amid allegations of referee favouritism that the team’s manager has dismissed as impossible to pull off in the VAR era. The petition, hosted on argentinaout.com, went viral recently after calling for the reigning world champions to be disqualified from the tournament in North America. Its organisers claim that Fifa and match officials have actively favoured Argentina and their captain, Lionel Messi. “It is obvious that Fifa and the referees are biased toward Lionel Messi and Argentina,” the petition read.

news.title: Combined XI: France vs. Spain, World Cup 2026
news.lede: With the semi-finals almost upon us, it is time to have some fun and look at what a combined XI of the teams involved would look like. First up is France vs Spain, two sides who boast some of the best talent in the world at each position. Making a lineup like this is not easy, and naturally involves some compromise. Note: The goal of making this XI is to have a plausible lineup that could actually work.

news.title: The nations with the most World Cup semi-final appearances
news.lede: Spain and Portugal are not among the top 10 nations with the most World Cup semi-final appearances. On the other hand, Sweden, Uruguay, and Croatia do indeed have a place in this pantheon of the final four. Many would think that mighty Brazil (8 semi-finals) tops this ranking. And yet, it is Germany that sits at the top with 12 appearances in the final four of the World Cup.

news.title: World Cup 2026: American Ismail Elfath to referee England v Argentina
news.lede: Ismail Elfath sent off Uruguay midfielder Agustin Canobbio in their defeat against Spain. Fifa has announced American referee Ismail Elfath will take charge of England's World Cup semi-final against Argentina. The 44-year-old officiated group games between Japan and the Netherlands and Uruguay versus Spain in the tournament, as well as Norway's last-16 victory over five-time winners Brazil. He has issued six yellow cards and shown Uruguay midfielder Agustin Canobbio a straight red card for a high challenge on Spain defender Pau Cubarsi.

news.title: World Cup hype makes him immortal! Incredible scenes in South America
news.lede: It wasn’t only since the World Cup that Erling Haaland has been drawing attention with his performances. But since the tournament in the United States, Canada, and Mexico at the latest, even football fans with no interest in the English Premier League know who he is. How huge the hype surrounding the Norwegian superstar has become is especially evident in Peru. As the civil registry announced, 468 children there bear the 25-year-old’s last name, according to a report by the news agency AFP.

news.title: The most expensive World Cup 2026 tickets ranked: World Cup Final, $30,000+ tickets, Argentina and more
news.lede: These are the whoppers on the World Cup ticket market right now, going as high as $30,000. The FIFA World Cup has officially returned to North America for the first time since USA '94. Thirty years ago, despite soccer's lower profile in the region, that tournament shattered attendance metrics to become one of the most commercially successful iterations in history. Now, the newly expanded 48-team tournament is deep into its high-stakes knockout stages.
